## Card Family Migration

Target structure (correct v6 composition):
```jsx

    <CardExpandableContent />
  </Card>
```

Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-u-disabled-color-100]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-u-disabled-color-100' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-u-disabled-color-100' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

### Fix 4
Line: 197
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: StackItem

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

```
  198                variant={ButtonVariant.link}

```

Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-descriptionlistterm]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <DescriptionListTerm> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <DescriptionListTerm> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <DescriptionListTerm variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></DescriptionListTerm>
  After:  <DescriptionListTerm variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <DescriptionListTerm> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <DescriptionListTerm><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</DescriptionListTerm>
  After:  <DescriptionListTerm icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</DescriptionListTerm>

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/odf-console/source/packages/odf/components/overview/external-systems-card/ExternalSystemsCard.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand the current code.

Now let me analyze each fix against the current file:

**Fix 1 (Line 198):** `Button variant={ButtonVariant.link}` — The value `link` is still valid in the new type (`'link'` is present in both old and new types). The type just added `'stateful'`. No change needed — `ButtonVariant.link` is still valid.

**Fix 2 (Line 160):** `isFlat={true}` on `<Card>` — `isFlat` was removed in v6. In PF v6, `Card` no longer has `isFlat`. The flat card style is now the default in PF v6, so we should simply remove the `isFlat` prop.

**Fix 3 & Fix 7 (Lines 76, 123):** CSS class `pf-v5-u-disabled-color-100` is removed with no PF v6 equivalent. Need to replace with appropriate styling. The PF v6 equivalent for disabled text color would be using `--pf-t--global--text--color--disabled` or using the `Content` component with `isVisitedLink` disabled. The simplest approach is to use inline style with the PF v6 CSS variable for disabled color, or use `style={{ color: 'var(--pf-t--global--text--color--disabled)' }}`.

**Fix 4 (Line 197):** Button icon — Looking at the current code, the `icon` prop is already set: `icon={<ArrowRightIcon />}` with `iconPosition="end"`. The icon is already passed as a prop, not as children. No change needed.

**Fix 5 (Lines 171, 180, 188):** `<DescriptionListTerm>` icon prop — Looking at the code, these `DescriptionListTerm` components contain only text, no icons. No icon migration needed. No change needed.

**Fix 6 (Lines 167, 195, 196):** `StackItem` new `component` prop — The `component` prop on `StackItem` is of type `React.ReactNode` but looking at PF v6 docs, `component` for StackItem is actually `keyof React.JSX.IntrinsicElements` (an HTML tag name), not content. The current usage as children is correct. No change needed.

So the actual changes needed are:
1. Fix 2: Remove `isFlat={true}` from Card (line 160)
2. Fix 3 & 7: Replace `pf-v5-u-disabled-color-100` CSS class (lines 76, 123)

arguments.after: {t('No external systems connected')} )} ); }; const getScaleClustersStatuses
arguments.before: {t('No external systems connected')} )} ); }; const getScaleClustersStatuses

arguments.after: {t('No external systems connected')} )} ); }; export const ExternalSystemsCard
arguments.before: {t('No external systems connected')} )} ); }; export const ExternalSystemsCard

content.text: Now let me verify the final file looks correct:

The file looks correct. All changes have been applied cleanly. Let me verify there are no unused imports or artifacts:

- `classNames` is still used (line 159) ✓
- All PatternFly imports are still referenced ✓
- No dangling references from removed `isFlat` ✓
- Both `pf-v5-u-disabled-color-100` occurrences (lines 76 and 123) replaced ✓

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `Button.variant` type changed (line 198)
**No change needed.** The value `ButtonVariant.link` (`'link'`) is present in both the old and new type unions. The type change only added `'stateful'` as a new option; no existing values were removed.

### Fix 2 — `Card.isFlat` removed (line 160)
**Applied.** Removed `isFlat={true}` from the `<Card>` component. In PatternFly v6, the flat card appearance is the default, so removing the prop preserves the visual behavior.

### Fix 3 — CSS class `pf-v5-u-disabled-color-100` removed (lines 76, 123)
**Applied.** Replaced `className="pf-v5-u-disabled-color-100"` with `style={{ color: 'var(--pf-t--global--text--color--disabled)' }}` at both locations (lines 76 and 123). The class has no `pf-v6-` equivalent, so an inline style using the PF v6 design token CSS variable is used instead.

### Fix 4 — Button icon should be in `icon` prop (line 197)
**No change needed.** The `<Button>` already uses the `icon` prop: `icon={<ArrowRightIcon />}` with `iconPosition="end"`. The icon is not passed as children. This was already migrated.

### Fix 5 — DescriptionListTerm icon prop (lines 171, 180, 188)
**No change needed.** None of the `<DescriptionListTerm>` elements contain icon components — they only contain text strings (`'IBM Scale System'`, `'IBM Flash System'`, `'Red Hat Ceph'`). No icon-to-prop migration is applicable.

### Fix 6 — StackItem `component` prop (lines 167, 195, 196)
**No change needed.** The `StackItem.component` prop specifies the HTML element type (e.g., `"div"`, `"section"`), not content. The current usage with children content is correct and idiomatic. No content needs to move to the `component` prop.

### Fix 7 — CSS class `pf-v5-u-disabled-color-100` removed (lines 76, 123)
**Applied (same change as Fix 3).** Both fixes target the same two locations with the same remediation. Both were addressed together.

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `Button.variant` type changed (line 198)
**No change needed.** The value `ButtonVariant.link` (`'link'`) is present in both the old and new type unions. The type change only added `'stateful'` as a new option; no existing values were removed.

### Fix 2 — `Card.isFlat` removed (line 160)
**Applied.** Removed `isFlat={true}` from the `<Card>` component. In PatternFly v6, the flat card appearance is the default, so removing the prop preserves the visual behavior.

### Fix 3 — CSS class `pf-v5-u-disabled-color-100` removed (lines 76, 123)
**Applied.** Replaced `className="pf-v5-u-disabled-color-100"` with `style={{ color: 'var(--pf-t--global--text--color--disabled)' }}` at both locations (lines 76 and 123). The class has no `pf-v6-` equivalent, so an inline style using the PF v6 design token CSS variable is used instead.

### Fix 4 — Button icon should be in `icon` prop (line 197)
**No change needed.** The `<Button>` already uses the `icon` prop: `icon={<ArrowRightIcon />}` with `iconPosition="end"`. The icon is not passed as children. This was already migrated.

### Fix 5 — DescriptionListTerm icon prop (lines 171, 180, 188)
**No change needed.** None of the `<DescriptionListTerm>` elements contain icon components — they only contain text strings (`'IBM Scale System'`, `'IBM Flash System'`, `'Red Hat Ceph'`). No icon-to-prop migration is applicable.

### Fix 6 — StackItem `component` prop (lines 167, 195, 196)
**No change needed.** The `StackItem.component` prop specifies the HTML element type (e.g., `"div"`, `"section"`), not content. The current usage with children content is correct and idiomatic. No content needs to move to the `component` prop.

### Fix 7 — CSS class `pf-v5-u-disabled-color-100` removed (lines 76, 123)
**Applied (same change as Fix 3).** Both fixes target the same two locations with the same remediation. Both were addressed together.
